title: SonarAuth: Using Around Device Sensing to Improve Smartwatch Behavioral Biometrics
venue: Adjunct Proceedings of the 2023 ACM International Joint Conference on Pervasive and Ubiquitous Computing & the 2023 ACM International Symposium on Wearable Computing (UbiComp/ISWC ’23 Adjunct)

method.input_representation: Sonar images (50x100) and concatenated motion/touch features (60x20)
method.training_strategy: Randomly split 23 attackers into 17 for training and 6 for testing; 6 blocks of user data for training, 2 for validation.

evaluation.context: Achieved using the multimodal classifier for single thumb tap
Achieved using the multimodal classifier for single thumb tap
Achieved using the multimodal classifier for single thumb tap

evaluation_protocol: Each user had a model trained against 17 attackers and tested against 6 remaining attackers.

reported_limitations: Peak EER of 6.41% is not sufficient for effective real-world authentication
Validated without separate multi-recall sessions to test diverse variations
Thumb tap is less preferred by users

usability.usability_issues: Thumb tap reported as least preferred due to requiring larger gross movements of the hand.

key_contribution: Proposed and evaluated the first around-device behavioral biometric system for smartwatches using active sonar
main_strength: Multimodal approach significantly improves performance over sonar-only
main_weakness: Performance (EER) is not yet at a commercial-grade security level
insights.notes_for_review: The study highlights that thumb taps provide the most distinctive features likely due to hand proximity and articulation differences.
